interpretation_scope: rsync-daemon-only post-sync semantics; raw evidence is not modified

content_consistency.basis: rsync --checksum itemize reports checksum/size/mtime SAME for 18/18 objects; this is rsync internal checksum equality, not a heuristic inference
content_consistency.independent_nas_sha256_deferred_reason: NAS is rsync-daemon-only in current environment; mount is unavailable, SSH/appliance shell is not authorized, and NAS-to-local pull is not authorized.

filesystem_metadata_consistency.basis: permission/group itemization differs for 18/18 objects under the strict rsync itemization check
filesystem_metadata_consistency.actual_values_deferred_reason: Existing raw evidence exposes p/g difference bits only. No new credentialed rsync command, mount, shell, pull, chmod, chgrp or normalization was executed while producing this interpretation layer.

strict_consistency.basis: current guardrail treats any itemized rsync line as mismatch; under that strict filesystem-attribute semantics, 18/18 comparisons remain mismatched

root_cause_assessment.most_likely_hypothesis: NAS rsync daemon or NAS filesystem policy may be overriding or refusing source-side permission/group parity, because the execute step already used rsync -aH --checksum and -a includes perms/group/owner.
needs_new_access_authorization_for: NAS rsync module configuration inspection outside rsync protocol

next_decision_boundary.current_no_risk_work: record interpretation layer and follow-up tracking only
next_decision_boundary.future_decision_required_before_closure: choose whether to keep strict metadata block, reclassify p/g parity out of Phase 1 content exit with follow-up tracking, accept READY_WITH_RISK metadata risk, or authorize a separate runtime gate
